product.name: ArWoWear T13 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 100% Cotton Satin, Flame Retardant Fabric, 520 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 520 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 5/2 Satin Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T13 Flame Pro is heavy weight 520 g/m², 100% cotton, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing. Suitable for shipyard welders in cold climates.
100% Cotton Satin, Flame Retardant Fabric, 520 gr/m'2
520 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 5/2 Satin Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>2400N; Weft>1800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>35N; Weft>30N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

evidence: Suitable for shipyard welders in cold climates
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal resistance, plus explicit statement of suitability for shipyard welders, directly support the welding application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Arc protection (EN 61482, ATPV >20 cal/cm²) suggests electrical hazard protection, but no explicit statement of energy-sector or electrical utility use.
Mentions shipyard welders (cold climates) which implies a maritime/shipyard context, but this is tied to welding occupation not vehicle/vessel transport use; not explicit transport application.

evidence: Suitable for shipyard welders in cold climates
primary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification, molten-metal resistance, and explicit statement of suitability for shipyard welders make welding the clearest and most direct end use.

evidence: EN ISO 11612 Flame and Heat Protection
categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics
secondary.rationale: The fabric is broadly marketed as general FR workwear coverall fabric with heat protection, supporting a secondary general industrial workwear use beyond the specific welding niche.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Arc protection (EN 61482, ATPV) is present but no explicit statement of electrical utility or energy-sector use, only a property-level certification.
Mention of shipyard context relates to welding occupation, not vehicle/vessel transport use.
No explicit firefighting or turnout gear reference despite FR properties.
